solution_text: Kernel is the core of OS that manages CPU, memory, and devices.
System calls allow programs to request services from OS.
A process is a program in execution.
A thread is the smallest unit of execution within a process.
Concurrency is execution of multiple tasks simultaneously.
Semaphore controls access to shared resources.
Deadlock is a situation where processes wait indefinitely.
CPU scheduling selects which process runs next.
FCFS executes processes in arrival order.
SJF selects shortest job first.
Round Robin gives equal time to each process.
Priority scheduling executes higher priority first.
Paging divides memory into pages.
Segmentation divides memory logically.
Virtual memory uses disk as RAM extension.
Page replacement replaces pages in memory.
Memory allocation assigns memory to processes.
I/O system handles device communication.
Disk scheduling orders disk requests.
RAID combines disks for reliability.
File system manages storage.
Directory organizes files.
An operating system (OS) manages hardware and software resources and provides an interface between user and computer.
